# Verify Events

> \[!NOTE]
>
> This Verify feature is currently in the Pilot maturity stage, which means that we're actively looking for early-adopter customers to try it out and give feedback. That could be you.
>
> Onboarding is now a self-serve process, follow our [Verify Events Onboarding Guide](/docs/verify/verify-events-webhook-onboarding-guide) for a step-by-step walkthrough.
>
> Verify Events currently only supports **SMS**, **WhatsApp**, **RCS**, and **Voice** channels.

Verify Events helps your business by providing instantaneous, real-time information on the activity of your Verify Service. It is supported by [Twilio Event Streams API](/docs/events), an API that allows you to tap into a unified stream of interactions across different Twilio products. Use Verify Events for granular visibility to track:

* Messages
  * Message status
  * Carrier network code
* Verifications
  * Verification statuses
  * Verification (OTP) success rate
  * Verification conversion rate

## Getting started with Event Streams API

Verify Events is powered by Twilio's Event Streams API. Learn more about [Event Streams](/docs/events).

You can stream Verify Events data to your existing systems by configuring a modern, persistent streaming technology like Amazon Kinesis or to a webhook.

[Amazon Kinesis](https://aws.amazon.com/kinesis/) is a data streaming platform where you can collect and process large streams of data in real time. Amazon Kinesis is within the AWS ecosystem, so its infrastructure is fully managed for you and it is easily scalable with low latency.

[Webhooks](/docs/glossary/what-is-a-webhook), or status callbacks, are HTTP requests that are triggered to send to a user-defined URL when events happen. With this option, Event Streams API will send either an `HTTP POST` or an `HTTP GET` request to the URL you specify. The request body will contain all of the information about that incoming Verify Event.

Onboarding onto Verify Events on Event Streams is now a self-serve process, follow our [Verify Events Onboarding Guide](/docs/verify/verify-events-webhook-onboarding-guide) for a step-by-step walkthrough.

## FAQ

### What should I consider when choosing between using Verification Attempts API and Verify Events?

[Verification Attempts API](/docs/verify/api/attempts) is a REST API that allows you to list and filter Verification Attempts generated by your Verify Service in the last 30 days. It is not real-time, but it can be used for reporting and troubleshooting.

Verify Events API is a real-time data stream that allows you to consume data about your Verifications using a streaming platform like Amazon Kinesis or with webhooks. It helps you to analyze granular data and build your own performance dashboards.

### What is the main difference between verification status and message status event streams?

Verification status events track the overall lifecycle of a verification from creation to resolution. An event is sent when a verification transitions between states: `PENDING`, `APPROVED`, `CANCELED`, `EXPIRED`, or `MAX\_ATTEMPTS\_REACHED`.

Message Status tracks the delivery outcome of messages sent during a verification. An event is sent for each message attempt with statuses like `SENT`, `DELIVERED`, `UNDELIVERED`, `READ`, and `FAILED`.

### Are there any cases where Verification status cannot be determined?

If you have custom codes enabled and are not using the [Update a Verification Status endpoint](/docs/verify/api/verification#update-a-verification-status) to report Verification status updates, Verify Events will not have verification status available.

### Are Message statuses provided close to the moment of delivery to the end device?

A message status is not always available right away if it is delayed from the provider. In that case, the Verify Events system will attempt to retrieve delivery status several times for up to 1 hour.

### How much does using Verify Events cost?

Verify Events are offered at no additional charge to Verify customers.
